title: GOOD: shlex+Command::new prevents shell injection in /! and bot hooks
description: Both cli/src/commands.rs (/! handler) and bot/src/scripting.rs (run_hook) use shlex::split + tokio::process::Command::new rather than sh -c. This is the correct approach: shlex parses shell quoting rules, Command::new execs the binary directly with no shell metacharacter interpretation. A security test in scripting.rs confirms that 'echo foo;cat /etc/passwd' passes 'foo;cat' as a single literal argument. The injection surface is eliminated, not mitigated.
